overall.description: Gameplay footage from GTA V showing a sunset walk along Vespucci Beach promenade. The character moves from an open beach area with palm trees past a skate park, then explores the commercial boardwalk with the Muscle Beach building and various souvenir shops.

scene_state.functional_zone: beachfront promenade with grass and palm trees

agents.ego_description: walking steadily along the beach promenade, passing benches and pedestrians with the sunset sky ahead

notable_agents.action: man in yellow shirt and purple cap walking past the camera
person in red pants walking across the promenade from right to left

entered.detail: tall palm trees lining the left side of the promenade against the sunset sky
grassy area with benches between the walkway and the beach
weathered concrete bench on the sidewalk near the grass
small pavilion structure visible in the distance near the beach

segments.scene_description: Walking along the open beach promenade at sunset, passing scattered pedestrians, palm trees, and benches. The grass lawn separates the walkway from the beach while the sky displays warm orange and pink hues.

scene_state.functional_zone: outdoor skate park with blue metal fencing

events.description: entering the skate park area with blue metal fencing and ramps
turning left toward the Muscle Beach building and commercial street

agents.ego_description: walking past the skate park and turning toward the Muscle Beach commercial area

notable_agents.action: person in dark hoodie walking ahead on the path toward the shops

entered.detail: bright blue metal mesh fence surrounding the skate park area
black banner with white text reading TEAM MUSCLETECH on the fence
concrete skate ramps and bowls visible behind the blue fence
tall red building with MUSCLE BEACH signage visible in the distance

segments.scene_description: Passing the blue-fenced skate park with MuscleTech banners and concrete ramps, transitioning from the open beach area toward the Muscle Beach building and commercial boardwalk.

scene_state.functional_zone: commercial boardwalk with fitness center and souvenir shops

events.description: entering the commercial strip with the Muscle Beach building and souvenir shops
camera tilts upward to view the Muscle Beach signage on the building facade

agents.ego_description: examining the Muscle Beach building facade and browsing the various souvenir shop fronts along the boardwalk

notable_agents.action: woman in pink bikini standing near The Green Doctors storefront
man with backpack walking along the shop fronts browsing items

entered.detail: black and red sign reading MUSCLE UP KINGS on the red building
large white sign with black text reading MUSCLE BEACH NUTRITION
storefront with colorful t-shirts hanging on display and surfboards
white display rack filled with sunglasses on the sidewalk
storefront with green neon sign reading THE GREEN DOCTORS and marijuana leaf symbol
teal colored building with WWW.SHOPOCEANBLUE.COM signage
white and green real estate sign reading DYNO FOR SALE with phone number

segments.scene_description: Exploring the commercial boardwalk with the red Muscle Beach building dominating the left side. The camera pans up to view the building signage, then moves past colorful souvenir shops displaying t-shirts, sunglasses, and beach gear, including The Green Doctors medical marijuana shop.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement along a coastal promenade that transitions from an open, natural beach setting into a dense commercial boardwalk. It begins with a walk past scattered pedestrians and palm trees under a warm orange and pink sunset sky, where a grassy lawn separates the walkway from the sand. As the camera advances, the open scenery shifts toward the blue-fenced skate park and its concrete ramps, moving steadily toward the urbanized section of the beach. The scene then expands into the commercial district, dominated by the red Muscle Beach building on the left, before the camera pans up to the signage. Finally, the movement continues past a row of colorful souvenir shops displaying beach gear and the storefront of The Green Doctors.
